m_Item1.QuestionText: Which CSS selector is used to select all elements of a specific type?
m_Item1.Explanation: The element (or type) selector selects all elements of a given type by using the element's name directly, like 'div' for all <div> elements.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What will the type selector 'p' select in an HTML document?
m_Item1.Explanation: The type selector 'p' selects all <p> elements in a document, which are paragraph elements.

m_Item1.QuestionText: How would you select all <h1> elements and set their font color to blue using an element selector in CSS?
m_Item1.Explanation: The type selector 'h1' is used directly to select all <h1> elements and apply the CSS properties, such as 'color: blue;' to them.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which character or keyword would you use in an element selector to apply styles universally to all HTML elements?
m_Item1.Explanation: The universal selector '*' applies styles to all elements on the webpage, unlike an element selector which targets specific types of elements.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following selectors has the highest specificity?
m_Item1.Explanation: The ID selector '#mainContent' has the highest specificity among the provided options, whereas the type selector 'div' and universal selector '*' have lower specificity.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the correct syntax to apply a CSS style to all elements with the 'highlight' class?
m_Item1.Explanation: The correct syntax for targeting elements with a specific class in CSS is to use a period (.) followed by the class name. Therefore, '.highlight' will select all elements with the class 'highlight'.

m_Item1.QuestionText: How would you apply a style to only the <p> elements with the class 'intro'?
m_Item1.Explanation: When applying styles to specific elements with a class, the correct syntax is to write the element name followed by a period and the class name. 'p.intro' targets only <p> elements with the class 'intro'.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following is NOT a valid use of the class selector in CSS?

Options.OptionText: Assigning a specific background color to multiple elements
Selecting an element with a unique identifier
Applying a font style to a group of elements
Styling a set of buttons in different parts of a webpage

m_Item1.Explanation: Class selectors are meant for applying styles to multiple elements, not for selecting an element with a unique identifier. For the latter, an ID selector should be used.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Consider the following HTML: <div class='container'></div>. What CSS will apply a border only to this element?
m_Item1.Explanation: To apply a style to elements with a certain class, the dot (.) notation is used in CSS. '.container' selects the element with the class 'container'.

m_Item1.QuestionText: In complex CSS selectors, what role does the class selector play?

Options.OptionText: It selects all elements of a specific type.
It works as a reusable selector for groups of elements.
It applies styles in a random manner.

m_Item1.Explanation: The class selector is used to apply the same styles to different elements across a webpage, making it reusable for groups of elements that should share the same styling.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the correct syntax for using an ID selector to style an HTML element?
m_Item1.Explanation: The correct syntax for an ID selector is to prefix the ID with a hash (#) followed by the element's ID name, then define the style in curly braces.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What distinguishes an ID selector from a class selector in CSS?

Options.OptionText: An ID selector is used for styling text only.
An ID selector can only be used once per page.
An ID selector allows for conditional styling.
An ID selector is always global.

m_Item1.Explanation: An ID should be unique within a page and used to style one specific element, unlike a class that can be applied to multiple elements.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following scenarios is most appropriate for using an ID selector?

Options.OptionText: Styling multiple paragraphs in a document.
Targeting a unique header for a single page.
Assigning styles to all buttons on a page.
Applying layout changes to sections and articles.

m_Item1.Explanation: An ID selector is ideal for targeting unique elements that need distinct styles typically used for a single or specific element on a page.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What happens if you use the same ID on multiple elements within a single page?

Options.OptionText: CSS will prioritize the first instance and ignore others.
Browsers will throw an error and stop rendering the page.
It could cause unexpected styling results and is not considered valid HTML.
All elements will be styled correctly without issue.

m_Item1.Explanation: Using the same ID on multiple elements violates the uniqueness rule of IDs in HTML and can cause styling conflicts or unexpected results.

m_Item1.QuestionText: How can you increase the specificity of a CSS selector that uses an ID?

Options.OptionText: Use multiple ID selectors separated by commas.
Use both the ID and class selectors together.
Prefix the ID selector with an element selector.
Wrap the ID selector in a higher specificity container.

m_Item1.Explanation: By combining an element selector with an ID selector, you increase the specificity without violating the uniqueness rule of the ID.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What does the universal selector in CSS target?

Options.OptionText: All elements within a specific section

m_Item1.Explanation: The universal selector (*) in CSS selects all elements on the page, applying styles to every HTML element by default.

m_Item1.QuestionText: How is the universal selector expressed in CSS?
m_Item1.Explanation: In CSS, the universal selector is denoted by an asterisk (*) and is used to select all elements within the document.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following is a typical use case for the CSS universal selector?

Options.OptionText: To apply a background color to the body only
To reset margin and padding of all elements
To select only HTML and body tags
To isolate specific elements for styling

m_Item1.Explanation: The universal selector is often used to reset properties like margin and padding across all elements to ensure consistent styling and layout.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is a potential downside of using the universal selector indiscriminately?

Options.OptionText: It can cause duplicate element IDs
It may increase the page loading time
It might override specific styles unintentionally

m_Item1.Explanation: Using the universal selector carelessly might result in unintentional style overrides, affecting specific elements' styles which might have been explicitly set.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which CSS syntax uses the universal selector to change the font style of all elements to italic?
m_Item1.Explanation: Using the universal selector in the syntax * { font-style: italic; } ensures that the font style of all elements is changed to italic.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which CSS selector would you use to select all anchor tags with an href attribute?
m_Item1.Explanation: The attribute selector 'a[href]' selects all anchor tags that have an href attribute, regardless of its value.

m_Item1.QuestionText: How would you select an input element with a type attribute that does not equal 'text'?
m_Item1.Explanation: The attribute selector 'input[type!='text']' selects input elements where the type attribute is present and not equal to 'text'.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the purpose of the CSS attribute selector 'a[href^="https"]'?

Options.OptionText: Select all anchor tags linking to a secure server.
Select anchor tags with href attributes that start with 'https'.
Select anchor tags with href attributes that contain 'https'.
Select anchor tags where href is 'https'.

m_Item1.Explanation: The attribute selector 'a[href^="https"]' selects anchor tags where the href attribute value starts with 'https', ensuring it's a secure link.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which attribute selector would allow you to select elements with a title attribute containing the substring 'intro'?
m_Item1.Explanation: The attribute selector '[title*='intro']' selects elements whose title attribute contains the substring 'intro' anywhere in the value.

m_Item1.QuestionText: If you want to style image elements with alt attributes ending with 'logo', which selector should you use?
m_Item1.Explanation: The attribute selector 'img[alt$='logo']' selects image elements where the alt attribute value ends with 'logo'.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following pseudo-classes would you use to select elements only when they are hovered over by the cursor?
m_Item1.Explanation: :hover is a pseudo-class used to select elements when the user designates them by hovering over them with a pointing device, like a mouse.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What does the :nth-child(2n) pseudo-class selector do?

Options.OptionText: Selects elements that are the second child of their parent
Selects elements that are at even positions among their siblings
Selects elements that are at odd positions among their siblings

m_Item1.Explanation: :nth-child(2n) selects every even-indexed child of their parent, based on a 0-based index: the 2nd, 4th, 6th, etc.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which pseudo-class is used to give a unique style to an element only when it is the current user location?
m_Item1.Explanation: :current pseudo-class can be used to style an element that is the current target location, intended for specifying the active target item.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which pseudo-class applies to form elements that are currently not empty when submitted?
m_Item1.Explanation: :valid pseudo-class applies to form elements whose contents are currently valid according to the input parameters and constraints.

m_Item1.QuestionText: For targeting the last child of its parent in CSS, which pseudo-class should be used?
m_Item1.Explanation: :last-child pseudo-class targets the last element that is a child of its parent, regardless of element type.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which combinator selector is used to select elements that are direct children of a specified element in CSS?
m_Item1.Explanation: The child combinator (>) is used to select elements that are direct children of a specified element.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the purpose of the adjacent sibling combinator in CSS?

Options.OptionText: To select elements that are not siblings
To select any sibling of a specified element
To select the first child of a parent element
To select an element that is immediately preceded by a specified element

m_Item1.Explanation: The adjacent sibling combinator (+) selects an element that is immediately preceded by a specified element, meaning they have the same parent and the selected element follows directly after the specified one.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which CSS selector is used to select all elements that are siblings, following a specified element?
m_Item1.Explanation: The general sibling combinator (~) selects all elements that are siblings of a specified element and appear after it in the HTML structure.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which symbol is used in CSS to represent the general sibling combinator?
m_Item1.Explanation: The tilde (~) symbol is used as the general sibling combinator in CSS, allowing selection of all siblings of a specified element that follow it.

m_Item1.QuestionText: When using the descendant combinator in CSS, which elements are affected by the selector?

Options.OptionText: All elements nested inside, regardless of nesting level
Only the first nested element of each type

m_Item1.Explanation: The descendant combinator (a space) selects all elements that are nested inside a specified element, regardless of their depth within the nesting hierarchy.

m_Item1.QuestionText: In CSS, what is the main purpose of using grouping selectors?

Options.OptionText: To apply the same styles to multiple selectors
To increase the specificity of a selector
To apply different styles to different elements

m_Item1.Explanation: Grouping selectors in CSS allows multiple selectors to be grouped together, sharing a common set of styles, which helps reduce redundancy and keeps CSS code cleaner.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following syntax is correct for grouping selectors in CSS?
m_Item1.Explanation: The correct syntax uses a comma to separate different selectors; in this case, h1 and h2 are grouped to share the same style.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What will be the effect of applying the following CSS: 'div, p { margin: 20px; }'?

Options.OptionText: Only div elements will have a 20px margin
Both div and p elements will have no margin
Both div and p elements will have a 20px margin
Only p elements will have a 20px margin

m_Item1.Explanation: Both div and p selectors are grouped, so they both will have a margin of 20px applied to them.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Why is it beneficial to use a grouping selector in a stylesheet?

Options.OptionText: It decreases the number of HTTP requests
It reduces code duplication and simplifies maintenance
It eliminates the need for external stylesheets
It makes the styles apply more quickly in the browser

m_Item1.Explanation: Grouping selectors helps to apply identical styles to multiple selectors, thereby reducing code duplication and simplifying future maintenance of the stylesheet.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following examples demonstrates improper use of grouping selectors?
m_Item1.Explanation: In the example '.class1 .class2, .class3 { font-size: 14px; }', '.class1 .class2' is a descendant selector, not properly grouped with other selectors, which can lead to confusion or errors in applying styles.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the primary purpose of using compound selectors in CSS?

Options.OptionText: To apply styles to an HTML element only when it matches all of the combined selectors.
To apply styles to multiple selectors at once, regardless of their hierarchy.
To apply styles to elements based on their attributes.
To change the specificity of the styles applied to an element.

m_Item1.Explanation: Compound selectors in CSS are used to apply styles to elements that match all of the combined selectors, enhancing specificity and precision.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following is a correct example of a compound selector in CSS?
m_Item1.Explanation: Option C is an example of a compound selector, combining two class selectors to apply styles to elements with the class 'box' that are also within an element with the class 'content'.

m_Item1.QuestionText: How does the compound selector '.card.highlight' behave in CSS?

Options.OptionText: It selects elements that have either the 'card' class or the 'highlight' class.
It selects elements that have both the 'card' and 'highlight' classes.
It selects the direct child elements of '.card' that have the 'highlight' class.
It selects all '.card' elements before any '.highlight' elements.

m_Item1.Explanation: The compound selector '.card.highlight' selects elements that have both 'card' and 'highlight' classes, not just one or the other.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Considering the CSS rule '.menu.active .item', what does it target?

Options.OptionText: All .item elements inside a .menu element or an .active element.
All .item elements inside an element with both .menu and .active classes.
All .menu and .item elements that are active.

m_Item1.Explanation: The selector '.menu.active .item' targets all '.item' elements inside an element that has both '.menu' and '.active' classes.

m_Item1.QuestionText: In the compound selector 'input[type="text"][required]', which elements are selected?

Options.OptionText: All input elements with a 'required' attribute.
All input elements that are of type 'text' and have the 'required' attribute.
All elements that are either input of type 'text' or have 'required' attribute.

m_Item1.Explanation: The compound selector 'input[type="text"][required]' selects input elements that are of type 'text' and also have the 'required' attribute.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following CSS selectors is used to select elements with a specific ID?
m_Item1.Explanation: The ID selector is used to select an element based on its ID attribute, using the # symbol followed by the ID name.

m_Item1.QuestionText: How do you select all elements of a particular class in CSS?
m_Item1.Explanation: The class selector in CSS is denoted by a period (.) followed by the class name.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which selector would select all paragraphs in a document?
m_Item1.Explanation: The element selector, specified with just the element name, selects all instances of that element type—in this case, paragraphs.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the syntax to select direct child elements in CSS?
m_Item1.Explanation: The child selector selects the direct children of an element and uses the '>' symbol.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which CSS selector selects all elements with the class name 'example' inside divs?
m_Item1.Explanation: The selector 'div .example' selects all elements with the class 'example' that are descendants of, but not necessarily direct children of, 'div'.

m_Item1.QuestionText: How do you select the last child of a parent in CSS?
m_Item1.Explanation: The ':last-child' pseudo-class is used to select the last child element of a parent element.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which CSS selector selects an element based on its attribute value?
m_Item1.Explanation: Attribute selectors, specified with square brackets, can select elements based on their attribute values.

m_Item1.QuestionText: How do you target all HTML elements on a page?
m_Item1.Explanation: The universal selector '*' selects all elements on the page.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which CSS selector is used to target an element immediately following another element?
m_Item1.Explanation: The adjacent sibling selector, denoted by the '+' symbol, selects an element that is immediately preceded by a specific element.

m_Item1.QuestionText: How would you select an anchor element with no specific class or ID?
m_Item1.Explanation: The element selector 'a' will select all anchor elements regardless of class or ID.

m_Item1.QuestionText:
In the below code snippet, in what order will the margins be added?
p {
  margin: 25px 50px 75px 100px;
}
m_Item1.Explanation: When 4 different values are added for margin property, it takes it in clickwise direction i.e. top, right, bottom and left

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following property is used to specify the transparency of HTML element
m_Item1.Explanation: opacity attribute specifies the tranparency of the element which can have values from 0.1 to 1.0

m_Item1.QuestionText: If we want to show an Arrow as cursor, then which value we will use ?
m_Item1.Explanation: cursor attribute can have different properties like default, arrow, crosshair, auto which will decide how the cursor should appear.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which CSS property is commonly used for displaying background images?
m_Item1.Explanation: background-image property helps in displaying background images.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What attribute can be used to select multiple elements in CSS selector?
m_Item1.Explanation: Class attribute is used like classification where multiple elements can have same class value.

m_Item1.QuestionText: How do you add a background color for all "<h1>" elements?
m_Item1.Explanation: To add background color to any element use following syntax : tagname {background-color:colorvalue}

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which CSS property controls the text size?
m_Item1.Explanation: font-size property specifies size of text.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the correct CSS syntax for making all the <p> elements bold?
m_Item1.Explanation: syntax is like tagname{attributename:value}

m_Item1.QuestionText: How do you display hyperlinks without an underline?
m_Item1.Explanation: a {text-decoration:none} to display hyperlinks without an underline .

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the correct HTML for inserting a background image?
m_Item1.Explanation: background-image is a style attribute which can be used to display body tag with backgroung image.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following selector selects all paragraph elements whose lang attribute  has a value of exactly "fr"?
m_Item1.Explanation: To select the elements based on attribute-value pair, [attr="value"] is used.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following selector matches all elements of a type?
m_Item1.Explanation: The type selector matches the specified type of elements.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following selector matches the all elements in the given document?
m_Item1.Explanation: The Universal Selector matches all the elements .

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following selector matches a particular element only when it lies inside a particular element?
m_Item1.Explanation: The Descendant Selector matches a particular element only when it lies inside a particular element .

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following selector matches a element based on its class attribute?
m_Item1.Explanation: The Class Selector matches a element based on its class attribute .

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following selector matches a element based on its id?
m_Item1.Explanation: The Id Selector matches a element based on its id .

m_Item1.QuestionText: How to apply a style to several specific element?

Options.OptionText: Use the type selector with , delimiter
Use the type selector with . delimiter
Use the ID selector with ; delimiter
Use the ID selector with > delimiter

m_Item1.Explanation: To apply a style to several specific element types use the type selector with, delimiter .

m_Item1.QuestionText: How do you make each word in a text start with a capital letter

Options.OptionText: You can't do that with CSS

m_Item1.Explanation: text-transform:capitalize to make each word in a text start with a capital letter .

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following property is used to set the width of an image?
m_Item1.Explanation: width property is used to set the width of an image .

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following property of a anchor element signifies unvisited hyperlinks?
m_Item1.Explanation: :link property of a anchor element signifies unvisited hyperlinks .

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following property is used to set the width of an image border?
m_Item1.Explanation: border property is used to set the width of an image border.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following property is used to set the height of an image?
m_Item1.Explanation: height property is used to set the height of an image .

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following property is used to set the shadow of a text?
m_Item1.Explanation: text-shadow property is used to set the text shadow around a text .

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following property is used to control the wrapping of the text within the element?
m_Item1.Explanation: The white-space property in CSS controls how white space (spaces, tabs, newlines) inside an element is handled. It affects how text wraps within the element and how whitespace characters are displayed.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following property is used to align the text of a document?
m_Item1.Explanation: text-alignc property is used to align the text of a document .

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following property is used to underline, overline, and strikethrough text?
m_Item1.Explanation: text-decoration property is used to underline, overline, and strikethrough text .

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following property is used to capitalize text or convert text to uppercase or lowercase letters?
m_Item1.Explanation: text-transform property is used to capitalize text or convert text to uppercase or lowercase letters .

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following property is used to set the color of a text?
m_Item1.Explanation: color property is used to set the color of a text .

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following property is used to set the text direction?
m_Item1.Explanation: direction property is used to set the text direction.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following property is used to add or subtract space between the letters that make up a word?
m_Item1.Explanation: letter-spacing property is used to add or subtract space between the letters that make up a word .

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following property is used to add or subtract space between the words of a sentence?
m_Item1.Explanation: word-spacing property is used to add or subtract space between the words of a sentence .

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following property specifies the top padding of an element?
m_Item1.Explanation: padding-top property specifies the top padding of an element .

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following property serves as shorthand for the padding properties?
m_Item1.Explanation: padding property serves as shorthand for the padding properties which allows to specify diff values to be used as padding for different sides.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following value of cursor shows it as crosshair or plus sign?
m_Item1.Explanation: crosshair value of cursor shows it as crosshair or plus sign .

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following value of cursor shows it as an arrow?
m_Item1.Explanation: default value of cursor shows it as an arrow .

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following value of cursor shows it as a pointing hand?
m_Item1.Explanation: pointer value of cursor shows it as a pointing hand.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following value of cursor shows it as the 'I' bar?
m_Item1.Explanation: move value of cursor shows it as the 'I' bar .

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following property specifies the color of a border?
m_Item1.Explanation: border-color property specifies the color of a border .

m_Item1.QuestionText: What does CSS stand for?
m_Item1.Explanation: CSS stands for Cascading Style Sheets .

m_Item1.QuestionText: Where in an HTML document is the correct place to refer to an external style sheet?

Options.OptionText: At the end of the document
At the top of the document

m_Item1.Explanation: Head section is the correct place to refer an external sheet.

m_Item1.QuestionText:
Which of the following property specifies whether a border should be solid, dashed line, double line, or
one of the other possible values?
m_Item1.Explanation:
:border-style property specifies whether a border should be solid, dashed line, double line, or
one of the other possible values .

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following property specifies the width of a border?
m_Item1.Explanation: :border-width property specifies the width of a border .

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following property changes the color of bottom border?
m_Item1.Explanation: :border-bottom-color property changes the color of bottom border .

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following property changes the style of top border?
m_Item1.Explanation: :border-top-style property changes the style of top border .

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following property changes the width of left border?
m_Item1.Explanation: :border-left-width property changes the width of left border .

m_Item1.QuestionText:
Which of the following property specifies a shorthand property for setting the margin properties in one
declaration?
m_Item1.Explanation: margin property specifies a shorthand property for setting the margin properties in one declaration where we can specify all 4 values .

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following property specifies the bottom margin of an element?
m_Item1.Explanation: margin-bottom property specifies the bottom margin of an element .

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following property allows you to control the shape or appearance of the marker of a list?
m_Item1.Explanation: list-style-type property allows you to control the shape or appearance of the marker of a list .

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following property specifies whether a long point that wraps to a second line should align with the first line or start underneath the start of the marker of a list?
m_Item1.Explanation: list-style-position property is designed to control the alignment of long list items that wrap to a second line relative to the marker.This property controls the positioning of the list item marker (bullet point or number) relative to the content.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following property specifies an image for the marker rather than a bullet point or number?
m_Item1.Explanation: list-style-image property specifies an image for the marker rather than a bullet point or number .

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following property serves as shorthand for the styling the markers in a list?
m_Item1.Explanation: list-style property serves as shorthand for the marker properties .

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following property specifies the distance between a marker and the text in the list?
m_Item1.Explanation: marker-offset property specifies the distance between a marker and the text in the list .

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following property specifies the bottom padding of an element?
m_Item1.Explanation: padding-bottom property specifies the bottom padding of an element .

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following property of a table element controls the placement of the table caption?
m_Item1.Explanation: caption-side property of a table element controls the placement of the table caption .

m_Item1.QuestionText:
Which of the following property of a table element specifies the width that should appear between table
cells?
m_Item1.Explanation: The border-spacing property in CSS is used to control the spacing between the borders of adjacent cells in a table. It applies only when the border-collapse property is set to separate (the default behavior).

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following property of a anchor element signifies visited hyperlinks?
m_Item1.Explanation: :visited property of a anchor element signifies visited hyperlinks .

m_Item1.QuestionText:
Which of the following property of a anchor element signifies an element that currently has the user's
mouse pointer hovering over it?
m_Item1.Explanation: :hover property of a anchor element signifies an element that currently has the user's mouse pointer hovering over it .

m_Item1.QuestionText:
Which of the following property of a anchor element signifies an element on which the user is currently
clicking?
m_Item1.Explanation: :active property of a anchor element signifies an element on which the user is currently clicking .

m_Item1.QuestionText:
Which of the following property of a table element specifies whether the browser should control the
appearance of the adjacent borders that touch each other or whether each cell should maintain its style?
m_Item1.Explanation: border-collapse property of a table element specifies whether the browser should control the appearance of the adjacent borders that touch each other or whether each cell should maintain its style .

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following property is used to draw a line around element's borders?
m_Item1.Explanation: Outlines do not take up space, they are drawn above the content.

m_Item1.QuestionText:
Choose the option that correctly specifies the output of the following CSS.
span {
    border: 1px solid red;
    outline: green dotted thick;
}

Options.OptionText: All span elements will have an outer red border and inner green dotted border
All span elements will have a green thick border and a red outline
All span elements will have a outer green dotted border and an inner red border

m_Item1.Explanation: The border property creates the inner border, while the outline sets the outer border.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following property specifies the color of an outline?
m_Item1.Explanation: As the property name specifies, it sets the color of the outline to the provided value

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following value specifies a dashed outline?
m_Item1.Explanation: The property defines a dashed border.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following property specifies the look and design of an outline?
m_Item1.Explanation: The outline-style CSS property is used to set the style of the outline of an element. An outline is a line that is drawn around elements, outside the border edge, to make the element stand out.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following values specifies a 3D grooved outline?
m_Item1.Explanation: groove specifies a 3D grooved border. The effect depends on the border-color value.

m_Item1.QuestionText:
Select the output of the following CSS.
div {
    border-width:5px;    
    border-style:dotted solid double dashed;
}

Options.OptionText: Box having dotted top outline, solid right outline, double bottom outline and dashed left outline
Box having dotted top outline, solid left outline, double bottom outline and dashed right outline
Box having dotted bottom outline, solid right outline, double top outline and dashed left outline
Box having dotted bottom outline, solid left outline, double top outline and dashed left outline

m_Item1.Explanation: The shorthand run in clockwise direction.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following specifies the width of an outline?
m_Item1.Explanation: The outline-width CSS property is used to set the width of the outline of an element.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following value specifies a solid outline?
m_Item1.Explanation: As the name suggests, a solid outline is created using this value.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following tag is used to place linked information?
m_Item1.Explanation: Linked information regarding CSS like external CSS document information is always placed in <head> tag. <body> tag contains the body of the document.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following tag can be used to embed a Document-wide styles in a document’s head element?
m_Item1.Explanation: Document-wide styles can be embedded in a document’s head element using the <style> tag. <script> </script> tag is used for Javascript.

m_Item1.QuestionText: The ________________ directive allows style sheets to be grouped and joined together, though some might wonder what the value of this function is given what linked styles provide.
m_Item1.Explanation: Within embedded <style> blocks, properties can be imported from an external file and expanded in place, similar to a macro. Importing can be used to include multiple style sheets. An imported style is defined within a <style> tag using @import followed optionally by a type value and a URL for the style sheet. <style> & <script> tag conatins CSS and Javascript content respectively. The metadata content is present in <head> tag.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following measurement defines a measurement relative to a font’s x-height?
m_Item1.Explanation: Defines a measurement relative to the height of a font in em spaces. Because an em unit is equivalent to the size of a given font, if you assign a font to 12pt, each em unit would be 12pt, thus 2em would be 24pt.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following measurement defines measurement as a percentage?
m_Item1.Explanation: Defines a measurement as a percentage. Percentages are denoted by a number followed by the % symbol and are always relative to another value such as length. Quite often they are used to specify some value relative to an inherited value from a parent element.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following property sets the font size of text?
m_Item1.Explanation:
Syntax:
font-size: length | percentage | larger | smaller | xx-small | x-small |
small | medium | large | x-large | xx-larger | inherit

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following property sets a variation of the specified or default font family?
m_Item1.Explanation:
Syntax:
font-variant: normal | small-caps | inherit
The small-caps value sets text in smaller-size all capitals. The normal value would be used to override any inherited font-variant value. Font-weight is for increasing/decreasing weight of font. It takes values like normal, bold, lighter, and bolder.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following property defines labels for a list of items?
m_Item1.Explanation:
Syntax:
list-style-type: disc | circle | square | decimal | decimal-leading-zero
list-style-image add graphical image to a list item. List-style sets all the properties for a list.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Suppose we want to arrange five DIV elements so that DIV4 is stacked on top of DIV1. Now, which css property will we use to control the order of stack?
m_Item1.Explanation: When HTML elements are overlapping with one another, z-index property will decide which element is completely visible. Element with highest z-index is completely visible

m_Item1.QuestionText: If we want to wrap a block of text around an image, which css property will we use ?
m_Item1.Explanation: float property is used to specify in which direction elements should float

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which css property you will use if you want to add some margin between a DIV's border and its inner text ?
m_Item1.Explanation: padding manages the space between element's border and element's content

m_Item1.QuestionText:
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
<head>
  <style>
    span {
      color: blue;
      border: 1px solid black;
    }

    .extra span {
      color: inherit;
    }
  </style>
</head>
<body>
   <div> Here <span> is a span element</span> which is pink, as span elements are set to  be.</div>
  <div class="extra" style="color: green"> Here is <span>a span element</span> which is green,
   because it inherits from its parent.</div>
  <div style="color: red"> Here is <span>a span element</span> which is red, as span elements are set to    inherit  the color.</div>
</body>
</html>

Which color is used to display span element in last div tag?
m_Item1.Explanation: span element in last div will acquire css written in internal style sheet for span tag

m_Item1.QuestionText: HTML classes that are already defined and allow us to apply styles on it are called as
m_Item1.Explanation: pseudo classes are already defined like hover, active, even etc which can be used by designers to directly apply the reqiured style. e.g a:hover, tr:even etc

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the key difference between display: none and visibility: hidden in CSS?

Options.OptionText: display: none hides the element but reserves its space, while visibility: hidden completely removes the element from layout
display: none completely removes the element from layout, while visibility: hidden hides the element but reserves its space
Both remove the element from layout and free up the space
Both hide the element but keep the space reserved

m_Item1.Explanation:
display: none - Completely removes element from layout (space is freed up)
visibility: hidden - Hides element but keeps its space occupied (layout unchanged)
Key difference: display: none affects layout, visibility: hidden only affects visibility.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following retains the element’s space in the layout?
m_Item1.Explanation: visibility: hidden keeps the element’s space; display: none removes it entirely.

m_Item1.QuestionText:
What is the output of this CSS?
.box {
  margin: 20px;
  padding: 10px;
}
m_Item1.Explanation: padding adds 10px inside the box; margin adds 20px outside.
